name: Glenn Osser
abstract: Abraham Arthur "Glenn" Osser (August 28, 1914 – April 29, 2014) was an American musician, musical arranger, orchestra leader, and songwriter. His birthname was Abraham (Abe), but much of his work was under the name Glenn; he can be found with references under both names.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder
about_quotes.evidence: I did that with Glenn Osser... Abe hired me just to play those things. It’s sort of a twist, but it was really nice. I did it for 22 years with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder
about_quotes.evidence: I did that with Glenn Osser [born Abe Osser]... I did it for 22 years with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder Part 2
about_quotes.evidence: Like when we did the Miss America pageant it was always a joy because he wrote such beautiful arrangements.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder Part 2 interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 12, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder Part 2
about_quotes.evidence: And Abe Osser was standing and looking, he couldn’t believe that this was happening, with a straight face.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder Part 2 interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 12, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: there's an arranger I like very much named Glenn Osser who's very capable in this sort of thing.
